title: penstemon pseudospectabilis subsp. clutei
intro: Penstemon pseudospectabilis subsp. clutei, also known as the Clute's beardtongue, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States, particularly in Arizona and New Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Clute's beardtongue include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of Clute's beardtongue can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's roots and leaves in a salve or tincture to treat various ailments.
